children.name: With the Mail application open, Click Mail and then Preferences in the top left corner of your screen. From the preferences pop-up, click the Signatures tab. Click the + button at the bottom of the middle column. Copy and paste your new email signature into the right-hand column and close the pop-up window to save.â¨ In this preview the images will appear blank. Don't worry â once you actaully go to compose an email the images will appear. Important: Â Ensure that "Always match my default message font" is unchecked, otherwise your signature will appear in plain text. Important: Â Still seeing a plain text version? Try toggling the "Place signature above quoted text" checkbox and pasting in your signature again.
children.content:
With the Mail application open, Click Mail and then Preferences in the top left corner of your screen.
From the preferences pop-up, click the Signatures tab.
Click the + button at the bottom of the middle column.
Copy and paste your new email signature into the right-hand column and close the pop-up window to save.â¨
In this preview the images will appear blank. Don't worry â once you actaully go to compose an email the images will appear.
Important: Â Ensure that "Always match my default message font" is unchecked, otherwise your signature will appear in plain text.
Important: Â Still seeing a plain text version? Try toggling the "Place signature above quoted text" checkbox and pasting in your signature again.

children.name: Select your new signature in the "Choose Signature" field near the bottom of the pop-up. Close the Preferences pop-up, compose a new email and enjoy your new signature! Important: Â Images not appearing when you compose an email? Go to your Privacy settings and ensure thatÂ Block All Remote ContentÂ is unchecked.
children.content:
Select your new signature in the "Choose Signature" field near the bottom of the pop-up.
Close the Preferences pop-up, compose a new email and enjoy your new signature!
Important: Â Images not appearing when you compose an email? Go to your Privacy settings and ensure thatÂ Block All Remote ContentÂ is unchecked.
